     1→/**
     2→ * Mermaid theme system inspired by beautiful-mermaid (lukilabs/beautiful-mermaid).
     3→ *
     4→ * Architecture:
     5→ *   - Two base colors: bg (background) + fg (foreground)
     6→ *   - All derived colors computed via color-mix ratios from bg + fg
     7→ *   - Produces a coherent mono hierarchy on any bg/fg combination
     8→ *
     9→ * @see https://github.com/lukilabs/beautiful-mermaid/blob/main/src/theme.ts
    10→ */
    11→
    12→interface MermaidThemeConfig {
    13→  theme: 'base'
    14→  themeVariables: Record<string, string>
    15→}
    16→
    17→// beautiful-mermaid MIX ratios (percentage of fg mixed into bg)
    18→const MIX = {
    19→  text: 100,
    20→  textSec: 60,
    21→  textMuted: 40,
    22→  textFaint: 25,
    23→  line: 50,
    24→  arrow: 85,
    25→  nodeFill: 3,
    26→  nodeStroke: 20,
    27→  groupHeader: 5,
    28→  innerStroke: 12,
    29→} as const
    30→
    31→function parseHex(hex: string): [number, number, number] {
    32→  return [
    33→    Number.parseInt(hex.slice(1, 3), 16),
    34→    Number.parseInt(hex.slice(3, 5), 16),
    35→    Number.parseInt(hex.slice(5, 7), 16),
    36→  ]
    37→}
    38→
    39→function toHex(r: number, g: number, b: number): string {
    40→  const clamp = (v: number) => Math.round(Math.max(0, Math.min(255, v)))
    41→  return `#${clamp(r).toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${clamp(g).toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}${clamp(b).toString(16).padStart(2, '0')}`
    42→}
    43→
    44→function mix(fg: string, bg: string, percent: number): string {
    45→  const [fR, fG, fB] = parseHex(fg)
    46→  const [bR, bG, bB] = parseHex(bg)
    47→  const p = percent / 100
    48→  return toHex(
    49→    fR * p + bR * (1 - p),
    50→    fG * p + bG * (1 - p),
    51→    fB * p + bB * (1 - p),
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→export interface ThemeTokens {
    56→  bg: string
    57→  fg: string
    58→  line: string
    59→  arrow: string
    60→  nodeFill: string
    61→  nodeStroke: string
    62→  groupHeader: string
    63→  innerStroke: string
    64→  textSec: string
    65→  textMuted: string
    66→  textFaint: string
    67→}
    68→
    69→export function deriveTokens(bg: string, fg: string): ThemeTokens {
    70→  return {
    71→    bg,
    72→    fg,
    73→    line: mix(fg, bg, MIX.line),
    74→    arrow: mix(fg, bg, MIX.arrow),
    75→    nodeFill: mix(fg, bg, MIX.nodeFill),
    76→    nodeStroke: mix(fg, bg, MIX.nodeStroke),
    77→    groupHeader: mix(fg, bg, MIX.groupHeader),
    78→    innerStroke: mix(fg, bg, MIX.innerStroke),
    79→    textSec: mix(fg, bg, MIX.textSec),
    80→    textMuted: mix(fg, bg, MIX.textMuted),
    81→    textFaint: mix(fg, bg, MIX.textFaint),
    82→  }
    83→}
    84→
    85→function buildTheme(bg: string, fg: string): MermaidThemeConfig {
    86→  const t = deriveTokens(bg, fg)
    87→
    88→  return {
    89→    theme: 'base',
    90→    themeVariables: {
    91→      background: t.bg,

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * SVG post-processing pipeline inspired by beautiful-mermaid-playground.
     3→ *
     4→ * After mermaid renders SVG, this module injects visual overrides:
     5→ *   - CSS custom property system for theming
     6→ *   - "Soft" subgraph styling (subtle fills, font weights)
     7→ *   - "Subtle" edge label styling (rounded bg, border)
     8→ *   - Corner rounding ("soft" preset)
     9→ *   - Consistent stroke widths and linecaps
    10→ *
    11→ * @see https://github.com/Justineo/beautiful-mermaid-playground
    12→ */
    13→
    14→import type { ThemeTokens } from './mermaid-theme'
    15→
    16→// Corner radius presets (matching beautiful-mermaid's "soft" preset)
    17→const CORNER_RADIUS = {
    18→  node: 5,
    19→  classNode: 4,
    20→  entity: 5,
    21→  subgraphOuter: 7,
    22→  subgraphHeader: 6,
    23→  actor: 6,
    24→  activation: 4,
    25→} as const
    26→
    27→function applyCornerRounding(doc: Document): void {
    28→  const applyRadius = (selector: string, radius: number) => {
    29→    const rects = doc.querySelectorAll<SVGRectElement>(selector)
    30→    for (const rect of rects) {
    31→      rect.setAttribute('rx', String(radius))
    32→      rect.setAttribute('ry', String(radius))
    33→    }
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  // Flowchart nodes
    37→  applyRadius('.node > rect', CORNER_RADIUS.node)
    38→  applyRadius('.node > .basic', CORNER_RADIUS.node)
    39→
    40→  // Class diagram nodes
    41→  applyRadius('.classGroup > rect', CORNER_RADIUS.classNode)
    42→
    43→  // ER diagram entities
    44→  applyRadius('.er > rect', CORNER_RADIUS.entity)
    45→
    46→  // Subgraph/cluster
    47→  applyRadius('.cluster > rect', CORNER_RADIUS.subgraphOuter)
    48→
    49→  // Sequence diagram actors
    50→  applyRadius('.actor', CORNER_RADIUS.actor)
    51→
    52→  // Activations
    53→  applyRadius(
    54→    '.activation0, .activation1, .activation2',
    55→    CORNER_RADIUS.activation,
    56→  )
    57→}
    58→
    59→function buildVisualCss(tokens: ThemeTokens): string {
    60→  return `
    61→    /* beautiful-mermaid inspired visual overrides */
    62→
    63→    /* Edge styling: rounded linecaps for clean connections */
    64→    .edgePath path.path,
    65→    .flowchart-link,
    66→    line[class*="messageLine"] {
    67→      stroke-linecap: round !important;
    68→      stroke-linejoin: round !important;
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    /* Cluster/subgraph "soft" styling */
    72→    .cluster > rect {
    73→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
    74→      fill-opacity: 0.96 !important;
    75→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
    76→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
    77→    }
    78→
    79→    /* Cluster title */
    80→    .cluster text,
    81→    .cluster .nodeLabel {
    82→      fill: ${tokens.textSec} !important;
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    /* Node stroke consistency */
    86→    .node rect,
    87→    .node circle,
    88→    .node ellipse,
    89→    .node polygon,
    90→    .node .basic {
    91→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
    92→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    /* Node fill */
    96→    .node rect,
    97→    .node .basic {
    98→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    /* Edge label "subtle" styling */
   102→    .edgeLabel rect,
   103→    .labelBkg {
   104→      rx: 5 !important;
   105→      ry: 5 !important;
   106→      fill: ${tokens.bg} !important;
   107→      stroke: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   108→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    .edgeLabel text,
   112→    .edgeLabel .edgeLabel {
   113→      fill: ${tokens.textMuted} !important;
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    /* Arrow heads */
   117→    marker path {
   118→      fill: ${tokens.arrow} !important;
   119→      stroke: ${tokens.arrow} !important;
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    /* Edge paths */
   123→    .edgePath path.path {
   124→      stroke: ${tokens.line} !important;
   125→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    /* Sequence diagram refinements */
   129→    .actor {
   130→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
   131→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
   132→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    .messageLine0,
   136→    .messageLine1 {
   137→      stroke: ${tokens.line} !important;
   138→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   139→    }
   140→
   141→    .messageText {
   142→      fill: ${tokens.fg} !important;
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    .loopLine {
   146→      stroke: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   147→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   148→    }
   149→
   150→    .labelBox {
   151→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
   152→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
   153→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    .loopText tspan,
   157→    .loopText {
   158→      fill: ${tokens.textSec} !important;
   159→    }
   160→
   161→    /* Activation bars */
   162→    .activation0,
   163→    .activation1,
   164→    .activation2 {
   165→      fill: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   166→      stroke: ${tokens.line} !important;
   167→    }
   168→
   169→    /* Note styling */
   170→    .note {
   171→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
   172→      stroke: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   173→    }
   174→
   175→    .noteText {
   176→      fill: ${tokens.fg} !important;
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    /* Inner divider lines */
   180→    .divider {
   181→      stroke: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   182→      stroke-width: 0.75px !important;
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    /* Class diagram */
   186→    .classGroup rect {
   187→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
   188→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
   189→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    .classGroup line {
   193→      stroke: ${tokens.innerStroke} !important;
   194→      stroke-width: 0.75px !important;
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    .classGroup text {
   198→      fill: ${tokens.fg} !important;
   199→    }
   200→
   201→    .classLabel .box {
   202→      fill: ${tokens.groupHeader} !important;
   203→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
   204→    }
   205→
   206→    .relation {
   207→      stroke: ${tokens.line} !important;
   208→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   209→    }
   210→
   211→    /* State diagram */
   212→    .stateGroup rect,
   213→    .statediagram-state rect {
   214→      fill: ${tokens.nodeFill} !important;
   215→      stroke: ${tokens.nodeStroke} !important;
   216→      stroke-width: 1px !important;
   217→      rx: 5 !important;
   218→      ry: 5 !important;
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    .stateGroup text {
   222→      fill: ${tokens.fg} !important;
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    /* Git graph */
   226→    .commit-id text {
   227→      fill: ${tokens.textMuted} !important;
   228→    }
   229→
   230→  `
   231→}
   232→
   233→export function postProcessMermaidSvg(
   234→  svg: string,
   235→  tokens: ThemeTokens,
   236→): string {
   237→  const parser = new DOMParser()
   238→  const doc = parser.parseFromString(svg, 'image/svg+xml')
   239→
   240→  const parseError = doc.querySelector('parsererror')
   241→  if (parseError) return svg
   242→
   243→  const root = doc.documentElement
   244→  if (!root || root.tagName.toLowerCase() !== 'svg') return svg
   245→
   246→  // 1. Apply corner rounding
   247→  applyCornerRounding(doc)
   248→
   249→  // 2. Inject visual CSS overrides
   250→  const existingOverride = root.querySelector('style[data-visual-overrides]')
   251→  existingOverride?.remove()
   252→
   253→  const styleEl = doc.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'style')
   254→  styleEl.dataset.visualOverrides = ''
   255→  styleEl.textContent = buildVisualCss(tokens)
   256→
   257→  // Insert style as first child for proper cascade
   258→  if (root.firstChild) {
   259→    root.insertBefore(styleEl, root.firstChild)
   260→  } else {
   261→    root.append(styleEl)
   262→  }
   263→
   264→  return new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(root)
   265→}
   266→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import type { ColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     3→import { useEffect, useId, useState } from 'react'
     4→
     5→import { darkTheme, darkTokens, lightTheme, lightTokens } from './mermaid-theme'
     6→import { postProcessMermaidSvg } from './svg-post-process'
     7→
     8→export function useMermaidRender(
     9→  content: string,
    10→  preferredColorScheme?: ColorScheme,
    11→) {
    12→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    13→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    14→  const [svg, setSvg] = useState('')
    15→  const [width, setWidth] = useState<number>()
    16→  const [height, setHeight] = useState<number>()
    17→
    18→  const colorScheme = useColorScheme()
    19→  const effectiveColorScheme = preferredColorScheme ?? colorScheme
    20→  const id = useId().split(':').join('')
    21→
    22→  useEffect(() => {
    23→    if (!content) return
    24→    setError('')
    25→    setLoading(true)
    26→
    27→    let cancelled = false
    28→
    29→    import('mermaid').then(async (mo) => {
    30→      const mermaid = mo.default
    31→      const themeConfig =
    32→        effectiveColorScheme === 'dark' ? darkTheme : lightTheme
    33→      const tokens = effectiveColorScheme === 'dark' ? darkTokens : lightTokens
    34→
    35→      mermaid.initialize({
    36→        startOnLoad: false,
    37→        theme: themeConfig.theme,
    38→        themeVariables: themeConfig.themeVariables,
    39→        darkMode: effectiveColorScheme === 'dark',
    40→        flowchart: {
    41→          htmlLabels: true,
    42→          curve: 'basis',
    43→          padding: 20,
    44→          nodeSpacing: 24,
    45→          rankSpacing: 48,
    46→        },
    47→        sequence: {
    48→          actorMargin: 80,
    49→          messageMargin: 40,
    50→        },
    51→        gantt: {
    52→          titleTopMargin: 16,
    53→          barHeight: 24,
    54→          barGap: 6,
    55→        },
    56→      })
    57→
    58→      let result

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Looking at the code, the `applyCornerRounding` function in `svg-post-process.ts` applies corner rounding to `.node > rect` and `.node > .basic` elements, but diamonds (rhombus shapes) in mermaid flowcharts use `<polygon>` elements, not `<rect>`. The CSS also styles `.node polygon` for stroke but doesn't add rounding to polygons.

SVG polygons don't support `rx`/`ry` attributes. To round a diamond shape, you'd need to either:
1. Convert the polygon path to a `<path>` with rounded corners


2. Use CSS `clip-path` or SVG filters

Since mermaid renders decision nodes as `<polygon>` elements, I'll need to post-process those polygon points and convert them to a `<path>` with rounded corners using bezier curves or arc commands.

需将 diamond 的 `<polygon>` 转为带圆角的 `<path>`。

    12→ */

    14→import type { ThemeTokens } from './mermaid-theme'

    18→  node: 5,
    19→  classNode: 4,
    20→  entity: 5,
    21→  subgraphOuter: 7,
    22→  subgraphHeader: 6,

    24→  activation: 4,
    25→  diamond: 4,

    28→/**
    29→ * Convert a diamond <polygon> to a <path> with rounded corners.
    30→ * Each corner is replaced by a quadratic bezier curve.
    31→ */
    32→function roundDiamondPolygons(doc: Document, radius: number): void {
    33→  const polygons = doc.querySelectorAll<SVGPolygonElement>(
    34→    '.node > polygon, .node polygon',
    35→  )
    36→  for (const polygon of polygons) {
    37→    const points = polygon.points
    38→    if (points.numberOfItems !== 4) continue
    39→
    40→    const pts: [number, number][] = []
    41→    for (let i = 0; i < points.numberOfItems; i++) {
    42→      const p = points.getItem(i)
    43→      pts.push([p.x, p.y])
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    const d = pts
    47→      .map((pt, i) => {
    48→        const prev = pts[(i + pts.length - 1) % pts.length]
    49→        const next = pts[(i + 1) % pts.length]
    50→
    51→        const dx1 = prev[0] - pt[0]
    52→        const dy1 = prev[1] - pt[1]
    53→        const len1 = Math.sqrt(dx1 * dx1 + dy1 * dy1)
    54→        const dx2 = next[0] - pt[0]
    55→        const dy2 = next[1] - pt[1]
    56→        const len2 = Math.sqrt(dx2 * dx2 + dy2 * dy2)
    57→
    58→        const r = Math.min(radius, len1 / 2, len2 / 2)
    59→
    60→        const startX = pt[0] + (dx1 / len1) * r
    61→        const startY = pt[1] + (dy1 / len1) * r
    62→        const endX = pt[0] + (dx2 / len2) * r
    63→        const endY = pt[1] + (dy2 / len2) * r
    64→
    65→        const moveOrLine = i === 0 ? 'M' : 'L'
    66→        return `${moveOrLine}${startX},${startY} Q${pt[0]},${pt[1]} ${endX},${endY}`

    76→        path.setAttribute(attr.name, attr.value)
    77→      }
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    polygon.replaceWith(path)
    81→  }
    82→}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 16:28:43Z)**_



Mermaid flowchart 中 polygon 类形状不止菱形。当前 `numberOfItems !== 4` 会漏掉六边形（6点）等。各形状情况：

| 形状 | 语法 | SVG 元素 | 圆角状态 |
|------|------|----------|----------|
| 矩形 `[text]` | rect | `<rect>` | ✓ 已处理 |
| 圆角矩形 `(text)` | rect+rx | `<rect>` | ✓ mermaid 自带 |
| 体育场 `([text])` | | `<rect>` | ✓ 自带 |
| 菱形 `{text}` | polygon 4点 | `<polygon>` | ✓ 刚加 |
| 六边形 `{{text}}` | polygon 6点 | `<polygon>` | ✗ 遗漏 |
| 平行四边形 `[/text/]` | polygon 4点 | `<polygon>` | ✓ 已覆盖 |
| 梯形 `[/text\]` | polygon 4点 | `<polygon>` | ✓ 已覆盖 |
| 非对称 `>text]` | polygon 5点 | `<polygon>` | ✗ 遗漏 |
| 圆形 `((text))` | `<circle>` | 天然圆 | — |

应去掉4点限制，对所有 polygon 通用处理。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 16:31:08Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants me to export `useMermaidRender` hook from the mermaid renderer package so the web app can reuse it. The current `MermaidRenderer` is tightly coupled to Vanilla Extract styles and `@haklex/rich-editor` (for `ColorSchemeContext`). 

For web app reuse, the key pieces are:
1. `useMermaidRender` hook - the core logic
2. `mermaid-theme` - theme configs
3. `svg-post-process` - post-processing

The problem is `useMermaidRender` uses `useColorScheme` from `@haklex/rich-editor`, which the web app doesn't use. 

Since the web app uses `useIsDark()` instead, I can export the hook and have them pass the color scheme as a parameter. The hook already accepts `preferredColorScheme?: ColorScheme`, so they can pass `'dark' | 'light'` directly and skip the `useColorScheme()` fallback entirely.

The real issue is that `useMermaidRender` depends on `useColorScheme` from `@haklex/rich-editor`, which won't be available if the web app doesn't use that context. So instead of exporting the hook as-is, I should extract the post-processing and theme utilities into standalone functions that don't require the rich-editor context, letting the web app compose its own solution. The issue is that `useColorScheme()` gets called even when `preferredColorScheme` is provided, so the hook still requires the context to exist—meaning the web app can't use it without wrapping everything in `ColorSchemeContext`. The cleanest solution is to extract the theme and post-processing utilities into a standalone entry point with no React dependencies, then let the web app integrate them into its own logic.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `MermaidRenderer` is already exported from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro` via `@haklex/rich-renderers`. But there's a problem - `MermaidRenderer` uses `useMermaidRender` which calls `useColorScheme()` from `@haklex/rich-editor`. This requires `ColorSchemeContext` to be provided.

The web app's `Mermaid.tsx` is used in a markdown rendering context (CodeBlock.tsx), not inside a `RichRenderer`/`RichEditor`. So there's no `ColorSchemeContext` provider.

But wait - `MermaidRenderer` accepts `{ content }` as props. 

Looking at the types, `MermaidRendererProps` comes from `@haklex/rich-editor` and the web app only needs `{ content: string }`. The real issue is that `useColorScheme()` would fail without context, though `useMermaidRender` does accept an optional `preferredColorScheme` parameter. The problem is `MermaidRenderer` doesn't expose this prop to pass through. I'd need to either wrap it in a context provider or modify the component to accept and forward the color scheme prop.

Let me check what `useColorScheme` actually returns when there's no provider—it might have a sensible fallback.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 16:33:38Z)**_

不能直接用。`MermaidRenderer` 内部调用 `useColorScheme()` 依赖 `ColorSchemeContext`，web 端的 markdown 渲染场景不在此 context 内。

有两条路：

1. **包一层 Provider** — 在 web 端用 `ColorSchemeContext.Provider` 包裹 `MermaidRenderer`，但引入额外依赖
2. **用 utils** — 即刚才的方案，web 端自行调 `postProcessMermaidSvg`

不过更好的方案是让 `MermaidRenderer` 支持外部传入 `colorScheme`，这样 web 端可直接复用。让我看看 `useMermaidRender` 已有 `preferredColorScheme` 参数，只需让 `MermaidRenderer` 透传即可。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 16:33:59Z)**_

`useColorScheme()` 默认返回 `'light'`，无 provider 不会报错。但有两个问题：

1. **暗色模式不生效** — 无 provider 时始终 `'light'`
2. **Vanilla Extract 样式** — `MermaidRenderer` 用的 CSS class 需要 `@haklex/rich-renderer-mermaid/style.css`，web 端用 Tailwind 体系

可行但需适配。最干净的方式：让 `MermaidRenderer` 支持 `colorScheme` prop 直传，web 端 dynamic import 即可。

     3→
     4→import {
     5→  darkTheme,
     6→  darkTokens,
     7→  lightTheme,
     8→  lightTokens,
     9→  postProcessMermaidSvg,
    10→} from '@haklex/rich-renderer-mermaid/utils'
    11→

    16→import { BlockLoading } from './BlockLoading'
    17→
    18→export const Mermaid: FC<{
    19→  content: string
    20→}> = (props) => {
    21→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    22→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    23→  const [svg, setSvg] = useState('')
    24→  const [width, setWidth] = useState<number>()
    25→  const [height, setHeight] = useState<number>()
    26→
    27→  const isDark = useIsDark()
    28→
    29→  const id = useId().split(':').join('')
    30→
    31→  useEffect(() => {
    32→    if (!props.content) {
    33→      return
    34→    }
    35→    setError('')
    36→    setLoading(true)
    37→
    38→    let isCanceled = false
    39→
    40→    import('mermaid').then(async (mo) => {
    41→      const mermaid = mo.default
    42→      const themeConfig = isDark ? darkTheme : lightTheme
    43→      const tokens = isDark ? darkTokens : lightTokens
    44→
    45→      mermaid.initialize({
    46→        startOnLoad: false,
    47→        theme: themeConfig.theme,
    48→        themeVariables: themeConfig.themeVariables,
    49→        darkMode: isDark,
    50→        flowchart: {
    51→          htmlLabels: true,
    52→          curve: 'basis',
    53→          padding: 20,
    54→          nodeSpacing: 24,
    55→          rankSpacing: 48,
    56→        },
    57→      })
    58→
    59→      let result
    60→      try {
    61→        result = await mermaid.render(`mermaid-${id}`, props.content)
    62→      } catch (err) {
    63→        document.getElementById(`dmermaid-${id}`)?.remove()
    64→        if (err instanceof Error) {
    65→          setError(err.message)
    66→        }
    67→        setSvg('')
    68→        setWidth(undefined)
    69→        setHeight(undefined)
    70→      }
    71→
    72→      if (isCanceled) return
    73→
    74→      if (result) {
    75→        const processedSvg = postProcessMermaidSvg(result.svg, tokens)
    76→        setSvg(processedSvg)
    77→
    78→        const match = processedSvg.match(/viewBox="[^"]*\s([\d.]+)\s([\d.]+)"/)
    79→        if (match?.[1] && match?.[2]) {
    80→          setWidth(Number.parseInt(match[1]))
    81→          setHeight(Number.parseInt(match[2]))
    82→        }
    83→        setError('')
    84→      }
    85→      setLoading(false)
    86→    })
    87→
    88→    return () => {
    89→      isCanceled = true
    90→    }
    91→  }, [id, props.content, isDark])
    92→  const { w } = useWrappedElementSize()
    93→
    94→  const encoder = new TextEncoder()
    95→  const data = encoder.encode(svg)
    96→  const base64EncodedString = btoa(String.fromCharCode(...new Uint8Array(data)))
    97→  const imgSrc = `data:image/svg+xml;base64,${base64EncodedString}`
    98→
    99→  return loading ? (
   100→    <BlockLoading>Mermaid Loading...</BlockLoading>
   101→  ) : svg ? (
   102→    <div>
   103→      <FixedZoomedImage

   105→        src={imgSrc}
   106→        width={width}
   107→        height={height}
   108→      />

   114→  )
   115→}

The user is right - `MermaidRenderer` uses Vanilla Extract CSS classes (`css.mermaidLoading`, `css.mermaidError`, `css.mermaidContainer`) which depend on `@haklex/rich-style-token` CSS variables. Without the theme provider wrapping it, those styles won't work properly in the web app context.

```tsx
import type { FC } from 'react'
import { useEffect, useId, useState } from 'react'

import {
  darkTheme,
  darkTokens,
  lightTheme,
  lightTokens,
  postProcessMermaidSvg,
} from '@haklex/rich-renderer-mermaid/utils'

import { useIsDark } from '~/hooks/common/use-is-dark'
import { useWrappedElementSize } from '~/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider'

import { FixedZoomedImage } from '../../ui/image'
import { BlockLoading } from './BlockLoading'

export const Mermaid: FC<{
  content: string
}> = (props) => {
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
  const [error, setError] = useState('')
  const [svg, setSvg] = useState('')
  const [width, setWidth] = useState<number>()
  const [height, setHeight] = useState<number>()

  const isDark = useIsDark()

  const id = useId().split(':').join('')

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!props.content) {
      return
    }
    setError('')
    setLoading(true)

    let isCanceled = false

    import('mermaid').then(async (mo) => {
      const mermaid = mo.default
      const themeConfig = isDark ? darkTheme : lightTheme
      const tokens = isDark ? darkTokens : lightTokens

      mermaid.initialize({
        startOnLoad: false,
        theme: themeConfig.theme,
        themeVariables: themeConfig.themeVariables,
        darkMode: isDark,
        flowchart: {
          htmlLabels: true,
          curve: 'basis',
          padding: 20,
          nodeSpacing: 24,
          rankSpacing: 48,
        },
      })

      let result
      try {
        result = await mermaid.render(`mermaid-${id}`, props.content)
      } catch (err) {
        document.getElementById(`dmermaid-${id}`)?.remove()
        if (err instanceof Error) {
          setError(err.message)
        }
        setSvg('')
        setWidth(undefined)
        setHeight(undefined)
      }

      if (isCanceled) return

      if (result) {
        const processedSvg = postProcessMermaidSvg(result.svg, tokens)
        setSvg(processedSvg)

        const match = processedSvg.match(/viewBox="[^"]*\s([\d.]+)\s([\d.]+)"/)
        if (match?.[1] && match?.[2]) {
          setWidth(Number.parseInt(match[1]))
          setHeight(Number.parseInt(match[2]))
        }
        setError('')
      }
      setLoading(false)
    })

    return () => {
      isCanceled = true
    }
  }, [id, props.content, isDark])
  const { w } = useWrappedElementSize()

  const encoder = new TextEncoder()
  const data = encoder.encode(svg)
  const base64EncodedString = btoa(String.fromCharCode(...new Uint8Array(data)))
  const imgSrc = `data:image/svg+xml;base64,${base64EncodedString}`

  return loading ? (
    <BlockLoading>Mermaid Loading...</BlockLoading>
  ) : svg ? (
    <div>
      <FixedZoomedImage
        containerWidth={w}
        src={imgSrc}
        width={width}
        height={height}
      />
    </div>
  ) : (
    <div className="flex min-h-[50px] items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-red-100 text-sm">
      {error || 'Error'}
    </div>
  )
}

```

```tsx
import { MermaidRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderers'
import { ColorSchemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-editor'

import { useIsDark } from '~/hooks/common/use-is-dark'

export const Mermaid = ({ content }: { content: string }) => {
  const isDark = useIsDark()
  return (
    <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}>
      <MermaidRenderer content={content} />
    </ColorSchemeProvider>
  )
}

```
